=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (22:14-22:30) TS377003: Missing errors ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC in the expected Effect type. effect(missingEffectError)
  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catchAll"

  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"

  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"

  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"

  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"

  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catchAll"

  (no quick fixes)

[D8] (39:28-39:37) TS377003: Missing errors ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC in the expected Effect type. effect(missingEffectError)
  Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catchAll"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catchAll" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError_plainAssignment.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Data from "effect/Data"
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.Error<{
  a: 1
}> {}

class ErrorB extends Data.Error<{
  a: 2
}> {}

class ErrorC extends Data.Error<{
  a: 3
}> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC>

export const noError: Effect.Effect<number> = Effect.succeed(1)

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingAllErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = Effect.catchAll(effectWithErrors, () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchAll not implemented"))

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingErrorC: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB> = effectWithErrors

export interface EffectSubtyping<A> extends Effect.Effect<A, ErrorA | ErrorB> {}

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingErrorCWithSubtyping: EffectSubtyping<number> = effectWithErrors

export function missingErrorWithGenericType<A>(error: A) {
  // @ts-expect-error
  const missingErrorA: Effect.Effect<never> = Effect.fail(error)
  return missingErrorA
}

// @ts-expect-error
const _ = effectWithErrors satisfies Effect.Effect<number, never, never>


=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

skipped by default

skipped by default

skipped by default

skipped by default

skipped by default

skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D6] Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catchAll" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError_plainAssignment.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Data from "effect/Data"
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.Error<{
  a: 1
}> {}

class ErrorB extends Data.Error<{
  a: 2
}> {}

class ErrorC extends Data.Error<{
  a: 3
}> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC>

export const noError: Effect.Effect<number> = Effect.succeed(1)

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingAllErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = effectWithErrors

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingErrorC: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB> = effectWithErrors

export interface EffectSubtyping<A> extends Effect.Effect<A, ErrorA | ErrorB> {}

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingErrorCWithSubtyping: EffectSubtyping<number> = effectWithErrors

export function missingErrorWithGenericType<A>(error: A) {
  // @ts-expect-error
  const missingErrorA: Effect.Effect<never> = Effect.catchAll(Effect.fail(error), () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchAll not implemented"))
  return missingErrorA
}

// @ts-expect-error
const _ = effectWithErrors satisfies Effect.Effect<number, never, never>


=== [D8] Fix 0: "Disable missingEffectError for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D8] Fix 1: "Disable missingEffectError for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D8] Fix 2: "Catch all errors with Effect.catchAll" ===

--- file:///.src/missingEffectError_plainAssignment.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import * as Data from "effect/Data"
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

class ErrorA extends Data.Error<{
  a: 1
}> {}

class ErrorB extends Data.Error<{
  a: 2
}> {}

class ErrorC extends Data.Error<{
  a: 3
}> {}

declare const effectWithErrors: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB | ErrorC>

export const noError: Effect.Effect<number> = Effect.succeed(1)

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingAllErrors: Effect.Effect<number> = effectWithErrors

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingErrorC: Effect.Effect<number, ErrorA | ErrorB> = effectWithErrors

export interface EffectSubtyping<A> extends Effect.Effect<A, ErrorA | ErrorB> {}

// @ts-expect-error
export const missingErrorCWithSubtyping: EffectSubtyping<number> = effectWithErrors

export function missingErrorWithGenericType<A>(error: A) {
  // @ts-expect-error
  const missingErrorA: Effect.Effect<never> = Effect.fail(error)
  return missingErrorA
}

// @ts-expect-error
const _ = Effect.catchAll(effectWithErrors, () => Effect.dieMessage("TODO: catchAll not implemented")) satisfies Effect.Effect<number, never, never>
